role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; one of three subjects of a joint oral history with Geraldine Crawford Bennett and Willie Elliot Jenkins (catalog entry 46, ingestion-only tier).
ai_reading: Toni Breaux shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 46 (catalog slug 'geraldine-crawford-bennett-toni-breaux-and-willie-elliot-jenkins'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Toni Breaux is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 46 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Toni Breaux; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Independent biographical sourcing for Toni Breaux is pending.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 46
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Robert (Bob) Hicks House in Bogalusa, Louisiana
gallery.caption: The Robert (Bob) Hicks House in Bogalusa, Louisiana, where the local Deacons for Defense and Justice chapter was formed in 1965. Breaux's joint oral history was recorded in Bogalusa.
